rq2.model_specification: Linear mixed model with LLM fixed effects and a persona random intercept: outcome ~ predictors + C(model_key), groups = persona_id, random intercept only. The same five LLMs evaluated all twelve personas, so LLM and persona are crossed; LLM-specific means are absorbed by fixed LLM indicators and persona-specific clustering is modeled by the random intercept.

description_lmm.note: Linear mixed model with LLM fixed effects and a persona random intercept: outcome ~ predictors + C(model_key), groups = persona_id, random intercept only. The same five LLMs evaluated all twelve personas, so LLM and persona are crossed; LLM-specific means are absorbed by fixed LLM indicators and persona-specific clustering is modeled by the random intercept.

inter_llm_agreement.sp_note: All SP description ratings were positive across all independent evaluations; inter-LLM disagreement was therefore undefined (zero variance).

nsp_fleiss.note: Fleiss' kappa on modal binary ratings across LLMs.

jaccard.primary_dimension_note: The LLM JSON output stores stereotype dimensions as unordered boolean fields; selection order is not preserved. A mutually exclusive primary-dimension inferential comparison therefore cannot be reproduced for the LLM data.

description_sp.apa: Exact agreement = 0.835; $\kappa = 0.000$; McNemar exact p < .001; discordant pairs = 0 (human Yes, GPT-5.4 No) and 42 (human No, GPT-5.4 Yes)

image_sp.apa: Exact agreement = 0.596; $\kappa = 0.219$; McNemar exact p < .001; discordant pairs = 26 (human Yes, GPT-5.4 No) and 77 (human No, GPT-5.4 Yes)

description_nsp.apa: Exact agreement = 0.490; $\kappa = -0.072$; McNemar exact p = .018; discordant pairs = 79 (human Yes, GPT-5.4 No) and 51 (human No, GPT-5.4 Yes)

image_nsp.apa: Exact agreement = 0.565; $\kappa = 0.126$; McNemar exact p = .022; discordant pairs = 43 (human Yes, GPT-5.4 No) and 68 (human No, GPT-5.4 Yes)

dimensions.apa: Age: exact agreement = 0.525; $\kappa = 0.000$; McNemar exact p < .001, Holm-corrected p < .001; discordant pairs = 0 (human selected, GPT-5.4 did not) and 121 (GPT-5.4 selected, human did not)
Gender: exact agreement = 0.541; $\kappa = 0.175$; McNemar exact p < .001, Holm-corrected p < .001; discordant pairs = 11 (human selected, GPT-5.4 did not) and 106 (GPT-5.4 selected, human did not)

Occupation: exact agreement = 0.525; $\kappa = 0.144$; McNemar exact p < .001, Holm-corrected p < .001; discordant pairs = 12 (human selected, GPT-5.4 did not) and 109 (GPT-5.4 selected, human did not)
Other: exact agreement = 0.788; $\kappa = 0.196$; McNemar exact p = .001, Holm-corrected p = .001; discordant pairs = 15 (human selected, GPT-5.4 did not) and 39 (GPT-5.4 selected, human did not)

stereo.primary_dimension_note: Mutually exclusive primary-dimension comparisons cannot be reproduced because selection order was not preserved in the GPT-5.4 mirror output.

dimensions.apa: Age: exact agreement = 0.576; $\kappa = -0.006$; McNemar exact p = .001, Holm-corrected p = .001; discordant pairs = 37 (human selected, GPT-5.4 did not) and 71 (GPT-5.4 selected, human did not)

Other: exact agreement = 0.941; $\kappa = 0.000$; McNemar exact p < .001, Holm-corrected p < .001; discordant pairs = 15 (human selected, GPT-5.4 did not) and 0 (GPT-5.4 selected, human did not)

non_stereo.primary_dimension_note: Mutually exclusive primary-dimension comparisons cannot be reproduced because selection order was not preserved in the GPT-5.4 mirror output.

usefulness.reason: Ordinal Likert outcomes were compared with a nonparametric paired test on pairs with non-missing human and GPT-5.4 responses. The mean paired-difference CI is a bootstrap CI for the mean paired difference among valid pairs.
usefulness.apa: $N_{matched} = 255$; $n_{valid} = 137$; $M_{human} = 2.64$, $SD = 1.31$; $M_{GPT\text{-}5.4} = 1.99$, $SD = 0.09$; mean paired difference $M_{diff} = -0.64$, 95\% bootstrap CI for mean paired difference $[-0.85, -0.42]$; Wilcoxon $W = 790.50$, $N_{nonzero} = 94$, p < .001, $r_{rb} = -0.823$; standardized mean difference $d_z = -0.49$

relatability.reason: Ordinal Likert outcomes were compared with a nonparametric paired test on pairs with non-missing human and GPT-5.4 responses. The mean paired-difference CI is a bootstrap CI for the mean paired difference among valid pairs.
relatability.apa: $N_{matched} = 255$; $n_{valid} = 137$; $M_{human} = 2.31$, $SD = 1.32$; $M_{GPT\text{-}5.4} = 1.05$, $SD = 0.22$; mean paired difference $M_{diff} = -1.26$, 95\% bootstrap CI for mean paired difference $[-1.49, -1.04]$; Wilcoxon $W = 18.50$, $N_{nonzero} = 84$, p < .001, $r_{rb} = -0.995$; standardized mean difference $d_z = -0.94$

relatability.reason: Ordinal Likert outcomes were compared with a nonparametric paired test on pairs with non-missing human and GPT-5.4 responses. The mean paired-difference CI is a bootstrap CI for the mean paired difference among valid pairs.

rq3.attribution_comparison_note: Paired McNemar tests compared binary dimension selections between human raters and GPT-5.4 mirror evaluations at the participant--persona level, with Holm correction across the four dimensions within each condition. Mutually exclusive primary-dimension comparisons cannot be reproduced because selection order was not preserved in the GPT-5.4 mirror output.
